title: Fix attribution pipeline hardening PR review gaps
summary: Code review of PR #18 (fix/attribution-pipeline-hardening) found two functional gaps and two nits. The PR correctly added a Step 0 validation gate and WROTE/NOT WRITTEN terminal lines to attribution-tracer.md, and correctly updated the bash path derivation in debug/SKILL.md. However, the same attribution-capture transcript_path fix was not applied to code-review/SKILL.md (leaving that skill broken), and the WROTE: <id> instruction in the Terminal output rule references the judge verdict JSON which does not contain an id field (the id comes from log-learning.py stdout). Two minor phrasing issues also need cleanup. Verdict: request changes. All findings are actionable and require no further judgment.
decisions: code-review/SKILL.md:226 must be updated: replace 'transcript_path: absolute path to the session transcript (always available)' with the same bash derivation block from debug/SKILL.md:298-303

code-review/SKILL.md:235 must be updated: replace 'transcript_path: from above' with 'transcript_path: the resolved absolute path from above (required — attribution-tracer will fail-fast if absent or unreadable)'

attribution-tracer.md:35 WROTE: <id> instruction must be corrected: the id is not in the judge's verdict JSON ({verdict, confidence, reason}); it is in the log-learning.py stdout line 'OK: appended learning entry (type=..., id=<uuid>) to ...' — instruction must direct the tracer to parse this stdout line
attribution-tracer.md:38 terminal output rule 'echo judge raw verdict JSON' must be scoped to cases 1 and 2 only (Step 0 failures never spawn a judge so there is no verdict JSON to echo)

attribution-tracer.md:57 Step 0 phrasing contradiction must be resolved: 'your entire response' conflicts with 'end your summary with NOT WRITTEN:' — unify to a single instruction that specifies the exact output

PR #18 core mechanisms (Step 0 fail-fast gate and WROTE/NOT WRITTEN terminal-line pattern) are well-designed as machine-parseable signals; the issue is the id source pointer in the WROTE instruction, not the pattern itself
bash derivation block in debug/SKILL.md:298-303 is correct and matches the actual path encoding used in the project (confirmed by memory directory layout)
next_action: Run /to-tasks to generate fix tasks for the four changes needed before PR #18 can be approved
problem_statement: PR #18 fixed the attribution pipeline for the debug skill but left the same broken assumption in code-review/SKILL.md. It also introduced a WROTE: <id> terminal line pattern where the id source instruction references the judge verdict JSON, which does not contain the id field.
solution: Apply the bash transcript_path derivation to code-review/SKILL.md, correct the WROTE: <id> source to reference log-learning.py stdout in the Terminal output rule section (line ~35), and fix two phrasing contradictions in attribution-tracer.md.
implementation_decisions: code-review/SKILL.md bash derivation block must be copied verbatim from debug/SKILL.md:298-303 (CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR + encoded_cwd + CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID)

attribution-tracer.md Terminal output rule (line ~35) WROTE: <id> instruction must reference: parse the 'OK: appended learning entry ...' stdout line from log-learning.py to extract the id, not the verdict JSON
attribution-tracer.md terminal output rule must add explicit scope: '(For cases 1 and 2 only — Step 0 failures have no judge verdict to echo)'
attribution-tracer.md Step 0 must unify to: 'Emit {"error": "transcript_path missing or unreadable: <path>"} as your only output. End with NOT WRITTEN: transcript_path missing or unreadable'

out_of_scope.text: Fix artifact-grounding-judge.md contradiction ('Do not return anything other than the single JSON object' vs 'Print the output from log-learning.py')
out_of_scope.rationale: Pre-existing inconsistency not introduced by PR #18; separate cleanup task.

out_of_scope.text: Audit other skills beyond debug and code-review for stale attribution-capture blocks
out_of_scope.rationale: Only two skills were found to have attribution-capture blocks; broader audit is a separate concern.

risks_and_tradeoffs: CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID may not be set in all execution contexts; if unset, Step 0 in attribution-tracer handles the resulting invalid path gracefully, so the risk is contained
